source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260119 MC, 23G 캐나다 등록서류 요청- IDC/260119/1. Quality Manual & Procedures/AQP706 Clinical Evaluation Regulation/AQP706_Clinical Evaluation Regulation.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: AQP706 defines the controlled clinical evaluation regulation for medical devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to plan, conduct, document, update, and report clinical evaluations and PMCF activities for medical devices.
keyPoints: Clinical evaluations must specify and justify the level of clinical evidence needed to demonstrate GSPR compliance.
Clinical evaluations are planned, conducted, documented, and updated across the device life cycle using clinical data, PMS, and PMCF.
The procedure defines responsibilities for the Quality Manager, Development Team, and Clinical Evaluation Officer.
The clinical evaluation process includes scope planning, clinical data identification, clinical data evaluation, analysis, and clinical evaluation report preparation.
Clinical evaluation reports must be dated, revision controlled, cross-referenced to supporting documentation, and signed by the evaluator.

entities.evidence: Complete revision in accordance with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements.

concepts.name: Clinical Evaluation
concepts.evidence: A systematic and planned process for continuously generating, collecting, analyzing, and evaluating clinical data on a medical device for the purpose of verifying the safety and performance, including clinical benefit, of the device when used as intended by the manufacturer

concepts.name: Clinical Evidence
concepts.evidence: Means the results of clinical evaluations and clinical data of sufficient quantity and quality for a device to enable a qualitative assessment of whether the device is safe and can achieve the intended clinical benefit when used as intended by the manufacturer.

concepts.name: Clinical data
concepts.evidence: Information on the use of medical devices and their safety or performance

concepts.name: General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR)
concepts.evidence: This regulation aims to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with the General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR) of medical devices manufactured by our company.

concepts.name: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up Plan
concepts.evidence: PMCF is a component of post-market surveillance (PMS) activities for medical devices.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance (PMS)
concepts.evidence: Even after the device is marketed, information collected from PMS activities should be periodically updated in clinical evaluations throughout the device's life cycle.

concepts.name: Equivalent device
concepts.evidence: A device proven to be equivalent to the device in question

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation report
concepts.evidence: A clinical evaluation report should be prepared to document the clinical evaluation and its results.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation plan
concepts.evidence: The clinical evaluation manager should develop and update a clinical evaluation plan

evidence.claim: The document is titled Clinical Evaluation Regulation.
The revision history states complete revision according to KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Complete revision in accordance with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements.

evidence.claim: The purpose is to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate GSPR compliance.
evidence.evidenceText: This regulation aims to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with the General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR) of medical devices manufactured by our company.

evidence.claim: The regulation applies to planning, continuously conducting, and documenting clinical evaluations of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: It is applied to planning, continuously conducting, and documenting clinical evaluations of medical devices.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluations must be planned, conducted, and documented according to MDR Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A.
evidence.evidenceText: The development team must plan, conduct and document clinical evaluations in accordance with Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A of MDR (EU) 2017/745

evidence.claim: The required clinical evidence level must be appropriate to device characteristics and intended purpose.
evidence.evidenceText: The level of clinical evidence must be appropriate considering the characteristics of the device and its intended purpose.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation documentation must be updated throughout the device life cycle using PMCF and PMS clinical data.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation and its documentation should be updated throughout the life cycle of the device with clinical data obtained from the implementation of the PMCF plan in accordance with Annex XIV Part B and the post-marketing surveillance (PMS) plan referred to in Article 84 of MDR (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report is part of technical documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation and its results and the clinical evidence derived therefrom shall be documented in a clinical evaluation report referred to in Section 4 of Annex XIV, which is part of the technical documentation related to the device.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation process includes Step 0 through Step 4.
evidence.evidenceText: Step 0: Defining the scope of clinical evaluation and establishing a plan; Step 1: Identification of clinical data; Step 2: Evaluation of clinical data; Step 3: Clinical data analysis; Step 4: Writing a clinical evaluation report

evidence.claim: Equivalence demonstrations consider technical, biological, and clinical characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: To demonstrate equivalence, the following technical, biological, and clinical characteristics should be considered.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation reports must have date and revision control.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation reports must have a date and revision control.

evidence.claim: PMCF is a continuous process for updating the clinical evaluation under MDR Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A.
evidence.evidenceText: PMCF should be understood as a continuous process of updating the clinical evaluation referred to in Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A of MDR (EU) 2017/745

evidence.claim: The related forms are Clinical Evaluation Plan 706-1 and Clinical Evaluation Report 706-2, managed by the Development Team for 10 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan 706-1 Development Team 10 years; Clinical Evaluation Report 706-2 Development Team 10 years
